name: Curried Frogs' Legs - Ech Nau Ca-ri
recipeIngredient: 1 stalk fresh lemon grass (or 1 tbsp dried lemon grass)
2 fresh red chilies, seeded and sliced
½ cup coconut milk or heavy cream
recipeInstructions:
This delicious lemon grass and coconut-laced curry comes from southern Vietnam. If you prefer a very hot curry, simply use more chile peppers.
1. Cut the frogs' legs into bite-size pieces. Rinse with cold water to remove any chipped bones. Pat dry and refrigerate.
2. If using fresh lemon grass, discard the outer leaves and upper half of the stalk. Cut into thin slices and finely chop. If using dried lemon grass, soak it in warm water for 1 hour. Drain and finely chop.
3. In a blender, combine the lemon grass with the chiles, shallots, garlic, sugar, curry paste, curry powder, salt, and 1 tablespoon of fish sauce. Process to a very fine paste. Rub the paste over the frogs' legs. Cover and refrigerate for 30 minutes.
4. Meanwhile, soak the cellophane noodles in warm water for 30 minutes. Drain and cut into 2-inch sections.
5. Heat oil in a saucepan over moderate heat. Add the onion and saute until translucent. Add the frogs' legs and brown well on all sides, about 3 minutes. Add the chicken broth and bring to a boil. Reduce heat, cover, and simmer for 15 minutes.
6. Uncover the pan and add the coconut milk, cornstarch diluted in 1 tablespoon of cold water, and the remaining 1 tablespoon fish sauce. Cook, stirring until the sauce thickens, about 15 minutes.
7. Add the cellophane noodles and bring the mixture to a boil. Remove from heat.
8. Sprinkle with black pepper and garnish with coriander sprigs.
9. Serve immediately with rice, French bread, or over rice noodles.
Yield: 4 servings
